  7413	                                                  VPCostContext &CostCtx,
  7414	                                                  Loop *TheLoop) {
  7415	  // First collect all instructions for the recipes in Plan.
  7416	  auto GetInstructionForCost = [](const VPRecipeBase *R) -> Instruction * {
  7417	    if (auto *S = dyn_cast<VPSingleDefRecipe>(R))
  7418	      return dyn_cast_or_null<Instruction>(S->getUnderlyingValue());
  7419	    if (auto *WidenMem = dyn_cast<VPWidenMemoryRecipe>(R))
  7420	      return &WidenMem->getIngredient();
  7421	    return nullptr;
  7422	  };
  7423	
  7424	  DenseSet<Instruction *> SeenInstrs;
  7425	  auto Iter = vp_depth_first_deep(Plan.getVectorLoopRegion()->getEntry());
  7426	  for (VPBasicBlock *VPBB : VPBlockUtils::blocksOnly<VPBasicBlock>(Iter)) {
  7427	    for (VPRecipeBase &R : *VPBB) {
  7428	      if (auto *IR = dyn_cast<VPInterleaveRecipe>(&R)) {
  7429	        auto *IG = IR->getInterleaveGroup();

  7431	        for (unsigned I = 0; I != NumMembers; ++I) {

/// LoopVectorizationLegality checks if it is legal to vectorize a loop, and
/// to what vectorization factor.

///   correctness of the program.

/// checks for a number of different conditions, such as the availability of a
/// single induction variable, that all types are supported and vectorize-able,
/// etc. This code reflects the capabilities of InnerLoopVectorizer.

/// induction variable and the different reduction variables.

/// LoopVectorizationCostModel - estimates the expected speedups due to
/// vectorization.
/// In many cases vectorization is not profitable. This can happen because of
/// a number of reasons. In this class we mainly attempt to predict the
/// expected speedup/slowdowns due to the supported instruction set. We use the
/// TargetTransformInfo to query the different backends for the cost of
/// different operations.

class LoopVectorizationLegality {
public:
  LoopVectorizationLegality(
      Loop *L, PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE, DominatorTree *DT,
      TargetTransformInfo *TTI, TargetLibraryInfo *TLI, Function *F,
      LoopAccessInfoManager &LAIs, LoopInfo *LI, OptimizationRemarkEmitter *ORE,
      LoopVectorizationRequirements *R, LoopVectorizeHints *H, DemandedBits *DB,
      AssumptionCache *AC, BlockFrequencyInfo *BFI, ProfileSummaryInfo *PSI)
      : TheLoop(L), LI(LI), PSE(PSE), TTI(TTI), TLI(TLI), DT(DT), LAIs(LAIs),
        ORE(ORE), Requirements(R), Hints(H), DB(DB), AC(AC), BFI(BFI),
        PSI(PSI) {}

  /// ReductionList contains the reduction descriptors for all
  /// of the reductions that were found in the loop.
  using ReductionList = MapVector<PHINode *, RecurrenceDescriptor>;

  /// InductionList saves induction variables and maps them to the
  /// induction descriptor.
  using InductionList = MapVector<PHINode *, InductionDescriptor>;

  /// RecurrenceSet contains the phi nodes that are recurrences other than
  /// inductions and reductions.
  using RecurrenceSet = SmallPtrSet<const PHINode *, 8>;

  /// Returns true if it is legal to vectorize this loop.
  /// This does not mean that it is profitable to vectorize this
  /// loop, only that it is legal to do so.
  /// Temporarily taking UseVPlanNativePath parameter. If true, take
  /// the new code path being implemented for outer loop vectorization
  /// (should be functional for inner loop vectorization) based on VPlan.
  /// If false, good old LV code.
</output>

The approach is:
1. Get the loop's latch block (the block that contains the backedge)
2. Find the exit condition in the latch block
3. Use ScalarEvolution to determine if the exit condition can ever be true
4. If it can never be true, report this as a vectorization failure

```
%ec = icmp eq i32 %iv, 0
```

  1990	bool LoopVectorizationLegality::canFoldTailByMasking() const {
  1991	
  1992	  LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "LV: checking if tail can be folded by masking.\n");
  1993	
  1994	  SmallPtrSet<const Value *, 8> ReductionLiveOuts;
  1995	
  1996	  for (const auto &Reduction : getReductionVars())
  1997	    ReductionLiveOuts.insert(Reduction.second.getLoopExitInstr());
  1998	
  1999	  // TODO: handle non-reduction outside users when tail is folded by masking.
  2000	  for (auto *AE : AllowedExit) {
  2001	    // Check that all users of allowed exit values are inside the loop or
  2002	    // are the live-out of a reduction.
  2003	    if (ReductionLiveOuts.count(AE))
  2004	      continue;
  2005	    for (User *U : AE->users()) {
  2006	      Instruction *UI = cast<Instruction>(U);
  2007	      if (TheLoop->contains(UI))
  2008	        continue;
  2009	      LLVM_DEBUG(
  2010	          dbgs()
  2011	          << "LV: Cannot fold tail by masking, loop has an outside user for "
  2012	          << *UI << "\n");
  2013	      return false;
  2014	    }
  2015	  }
  2016	
  2017	  for (const auto &Entry : getInductionVars()) {
  2018	    PHINode *OrigPhi = Entry.first;
  2019	    for (User *U : OrigPhi->users()) {
  2020	      auto *UI = cast<Instruction>(U);
  2021	      if (!TheLoop->contains(UI)) {
  2022	        LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "LV: Cannot fold tail by masking, loop IV has an "
  2023	                             "outside user for "
  2024	                          << *UI << "\n");
  2025	        return false;
  2026	      }
  2027	    }
  2028	  }
  2029	
  2030	  // The list of pointers that we can safely read and write to remains empty.
  2031	  SmallPtrSet<Value *, 8> SafePointers;
  2032	
  2033	  // Check all blocks for predication, including those that ordinarily do not
  2034	  // need predication such as the header block.
  2035	  SmallPtrSet<const Instruction *, 8> TmpMaskedOp;
  2036	  for (BasicBlock *BB : TheLoop->blocks()) {
  2037	    if (!blockCanBePredicated(BB, SafePointers, TmpMaskedOp)) {
  2038	      LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "LV: Cannot fold tail by masking.\n");
  2039	      return false;
  2040	    }
  2041	  }
  2042	
  2043	  LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "LV: can fold tail by masking.\n");
  2044	
  2045	  return true;
  2046	}

  2048	void LoopVectorizationLegality::prepareToFoldTailByMasking() {
  2049	  // The list of pointers that we can safely read and write to remains empty.
  2050	  SmallPtrSet<Value *, 8> SafePointers;
  2051	
  2052	  // Mark all blocks for predication, including those that ordinarily do not

Since I cannot modify assertions, and my attempts to prevent vectorization of infinite loops are causing compilation errors, let me try a simpler approach.

The key insight is that the loop in the reproducer has an induction variable that starts at 1 and increments, with an exit condition checking if it equals 0. This should be caught by the existing legality checks.

Let me check if there's a simpler way to detect this. Maybe I can add a check in the `canVectorizeLoopCFG` function that uses the existing ScalarEvolution infrastructure to check if the loop has a valid trip count.

Actually, looking back at the error, the issue might be that the loop vectorizer is trying to vectorize a loop that has no valid trip count. The `getSmallConstantTripCount` function returns 0 for such loops.

Let me try a different approach: instead of complex SCEV analysis, just check if the loop has a computable trip count. If not, mark it as non-vectorizable.

```bash
# Restore from backup
cp /tmp/legality_backup.cpp /llvm-autofix-deps/llvm/llvm/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/LoopVectorizationLegality.cpp

# Find the correct location again
START_LINE=1540
END_LINE=1660

# Get lines before the function
head -n $((START_LINE - 1)) /llvm-autofix-deps/llvm/llvm/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/LoopVectorizationLegality.cpp > /tmp/fixed.cpp

# Add the function start
sed -n "${START_LINE},$((END_LINE - 2))p" /llvm-autofix-deps/llvm/llvm/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/LoopVectorizationLegality.cpp >> /tmp/fixed.cpp

# Add the infinite loop check
cat << 'EOF' >> /tmp/fixed.cpp
  // Check for loops with unknown trip count (likely infinite)
  if (PSE.getSE()->getSmallConstantTripCount(Lp) == 0) {
    reportVectorizationFailure("Loop has unknown trip count",
        "loop control flow is not understood by vectorizer",
        "UnknownTripCount", ORE, TheLoop);
    if (DoExtraAnalysis)
      Result = false;
    else
      return false;
  }
EOF
```

